summary: The repeated indirect CALL R10 cleanup pattern in the login session open/close builders is now recognized by Reverser directly. Running the new pe-small-string-cleanup helper over 0x14062A760..0x14062AB6E and 0x14062AC81..0x14062AF1B recovered four high-confidence stack small-string heap cleanup dispatches through the global allocator service at 0x140E57B40.
confidence: high for the four recovered cleanup dispatches because each hit has a stack small-string high-bit sentinel gate, a non-null heap pointer load into RDX, the allocator-service load from 0x140E57B40, method dispatch through vtable slot +0x20 into R10, final RCX=R9 pool setup, and R8D size-source selection.

cleanup_calls.meaning: Heap-backed temporary small string cleanup inside the session/open builder, not an OTL transport submit.
Second high-confidence session/open temporary cleanup path through the same allocator-vtable free shape.
Heap-backed close body string cleanup after submit.
Heap-backed close endpoint string cleanup after submit.

shared_pattern.pool_selection: R9 selected from allocator service +0x108/+0x100 fallback to +0x198
shared_pattern.method_dispatch: R10 loaded from [[pool]+0x20], then CALL R10

reverser_tool_update.what_it_does: Scans PE function ranges for stack small-string heap cleanup dispatches and emits callsite, sentinel, released pointer, allocator service, vtable slot, size-source, and compact evidence records.

opennxt_947_server_implications: The recovered CALL R10 sites are client-side temporary string cleanup and should not be modeled as extra session open/close network submissions.
The session/open and session/close request submitter contract remains centered on 0x14062BCE0; cleanup occurs after endpoint/body construction and submit-side handoff.
When comparing OpenNXT traces against client behavior, endpoint/body string lifetimes can be ignored for protocol parity except when diagnosing client-side memory/state bugs.
next_targets: Run pe-small-string-cleanup over the remaining OTL auth, pending-entry, telemetry, and directlogin request builders to separate cleanup calls from callback or transport dispatches.
Use the helper output to remove manual ambiguity around indirect CALL R10 sites before naming request-builder control flow.
If the allocator-service shape appears outside OTL builders, consider a broader allocator/free classifier keyed on 0x140E57B40 and vtable slot +0x20.
